Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOSE MORENO's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against GMP Cars, LLC.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED as follows. No timely opposition filed. There is a history of failures to comply with discovery or this Court's orders, and there is no evidence that would show less severe sanctions would produce compliance with discovery. See Moofly Productions LLC v. Favila (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 1, 10-12 (collecting cases). Defendant GMP Cars LLC's answer is STRICKEN. CCP 2023.030(d)(1). The Clerk is directed to default as to Defendant GMP Cars LLC. The request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $3,310, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. I decline to enter default judgment at this point. Rather, Plaintiff may file a motion for default judgment once the default is entered. This is to provide Defendant a final opportunity to avoid default judgment.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gmp Cars Collision To Respond To Plaintiff Jose Moreno'S Requests For Production Of Documents Set One
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOSE MORENO's Motion To Compel Defendant GMP Cars Collision To Respond To Plaintiff Jose Moreno's Requests For Production Of Documents Set One.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion GRANTED. Discovery requests were served by U.S. mail on September 29, 2020. This mean that responses were due by early November 2020 (including time for mailing). According to the declaration, Plaintiff's counsel did not send a meet-and-confer letter until January 26, 2021. Because there is no evidence the parties agreed to extend the deadline, this was late. Nevertheless, CCP 2031.300 provides an automatic waiver of objections unless the non-responding/late responding party shows entitlement by waiver. Defendant did not timely object to this motion. Therefore, all objections are ordered waived. Defendant must produce all requested documents, without objection, no later than October 1, 2021. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $4,185. Sanctions payable by October 1, 2021.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.
